title: Ultrafilters in Arithmetic Dynamics
speaker: Jit Wu Yap
abstract: Given a one-parameter degenerating family of rational maps on the projective line, it is possible to construct a non-archimedean limit which captures how this family degenerates.  Recently, Luo used ultrafilters to construct limits for an arbitrary sequence of rational maps. This has been recently formalized by Favre-Gong using Berkovich spaces. In this talk, I will explain how to use Favre-Gong's theory to obtain (ineffective) uniformity results in arithmetic dynamics. In particular, I will prove that the number of common preperiodic points of two distinct monic degree d polynomials is uniformly bounded in terms of d, proving a conjecture of DeMarco-Krieger-Ye for monic polynomials.
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Uniform common-preperiodic-point problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up rational maps and preperiodic points, recalls the Baker-DeMarco finite-or-equal dichotomy, states the DeMarco-Krieger uniform boundedness conjecture, and announces the monic degree-d polynomial case obtained by ultrafilter methods.

proof_stages.label: Julia sets and equilibrium measures
proof_stages.summary: For polynomials, the filled Julia set is described via inverse images of a large disk, the escape-rate Green function is introduced, and the equilibrium measure mu_f is defined as its Laplacian with natural invariance properties.

proof_stages.label: Equidistribution forces equality of measures
proof_stages.summary: Galois invariance of preperiodic points and arithmetic equidistribution of their conjugates imply that an infinite common preperiodic set gives mu_f=mu_g, which for polynomials recovers the qualitative Baker-DeMarco conclusion and later converts energy bounds into uniform point bounds.

proof_stages.label: Energy pairing and quantitative lower bound
proof_stages.summary: An energy pairing between equilibrium measures is introduced as a distance-like quantity, and the desired uniformity is reduced to a height-proportional lower bound obtained by summing local archimedean and nonarchimedean/Berkovich contributions.

proof_stages.label: Pointwise separation over valued fields
proof_stages.summary: The key non-uniform theorem is that over any characteristic-zero valued field, distinct monic degree-d polynomials have distinct equilibrium measures and hence positive energy pairing, a statement surprising in the nonarchimedean setting and false for general rational maps.

proof_stages.label: Ultrafilter/Robinson-field degeneration
proof_stages.summary: For a degenerating sequence of polynomial pairs, a scaled sequence algebra is formed, its Berkovich spectrum is related to ultrafilters, quotient fields H_omega and limit polynomials f_omega are constructed, and normalized energy pairings are shown to converge to the limit pairing.

proof_stages.label: Compactness contradiction proving uniformity
proof_stages.summary: Assuming no uniform lower bound produces a sequence whose ultralimit has f_omega not equal to g_omega but zero energy pairing, contradicting pointwise separation; combined with equidistribution, this gives the announced uniform bound on common preperiodic points.
